title: Enforce guide action-contract
description: Create a formal contract and validator for workflow/guides so guide files must stay action-oriented and cannot drift back into explanation-heavy reference prose. The mechanism should live in the core docs and an executable validator script that can flag new or modified guide files that violate the contract.
close_reason: Added guide contract, validator, and entrypoint references to keep workflow/guides action-oriented

title: Refactor FlowForge skill to routing-first
description: Update agents/skills/flowforge/SKILL.md so it only names the action routing contract and the single rule-loading script, instead of enumerating a full load order. The skill should guide the agent to route by scenario first, then invoke the loader script to fetch the right core and project rules.

title: Validate routing layer references
description: After introducing the agent action routing layer, update references in the core docs and validate that no stale wording still implies the skill must list a full rule load order. Check links, command wording, and any documentation that should point to the new routing contract.

title: Define agent action routing layer
description: Design the first-layer agent behavior contract for FlowForge: identify scenarios, decide the required actions, and specify when to create or update artifacts before loading deeper rules. This contract should sit above the detailed workflow guides and define what the agent does before any domain-specific rules are loaded.

title: Rewrite FlowForge docs in Chinese-first prose
description: Rewrite the user-facing prose in the core FlowForge docs and key entry docs so Chinese becomes the primary descriptive language while preserving English for machine-facing contracts such as paths, frontmatter keys, schema fields, and command names. Focus on workflow/ guides, workflow README, and the main architecture/template entry docs.

title: Rewrite core FlowForge guides
description: Rewrite the selected workflow/guides files to remove stage-heavy workflow framing and keep only the core knowledge-base structure, document contracts, routing rules, and Agent reasoning skeletons that belong in the platform core.

title: Validate guides references and stale links
description: After the guide rewrite, update any README, template, or architecture references that point to moved or renamed files, then run diff and link sanity checks to ensure the new guide layout is consistent.

title: Map FlowForge guides taxonomy
description: Review workflow/guides and classify each file into: keep as core knowledge-base spec, rewrite as Agent reasoning skeleton, move out of guides, or merge into another guide. Produce the target structure before any content rewrite so the refactor stays coherent.

title: Localize FlowForge docs to Chinese-first
description: Update the project documentation language policy so Chinese becomes the primary human-facing language for this repository while preserving English for machine-facing contracts such as paths, frontmatter keys, schema fields, and command names. Then update the core overview docs to reflect the new policy.

title: Simplify project AGENTS.md
description: Reduce workflow/templates/project/AGENTS.md to a concise project-local entry contract. Remove duplicated workflow doctrine, keep only the minimal instructions needed for Codex to find the canonical workflow, project rules bundle, and local document roots.

title: Refine FlowForge workflow core
description: Review and tighten the canonical workflow under workflow/. Focus on reducing duplication, clarifying layer boundaries between guides, templates, and project rules, and removing any instructions that belong in thinner entrypoints instead of the core spec.

title: Optimize FlowForge skill
description: Refactor agents/skills/flowforge/SKILL.md to follow skill best practices: keep the skill thin, move detailed reference material out of the skill body, and ensure the entrypoint reliably guides the agent through the canonical workflow without duplicating core guides.

title: Make approve optional and have apply enter execution
description: Update FlowForge lifecycle so approve is optional and apply materializes tasks then starts implementation work by claiming ready Beads tasks.

title: Separate classification mechanics from project rules
description: Move generic exploration classification fields and target derivation guidance into FlowForge core docs, leaving installed project rules as project-level classification configuration only.

title: Add FlowForge exploration classification seed rule
description: Add a default project rule that classifies newly discovered exploration information into module, architecture, convention, decision, or exploration buckets, including module_name-driven routing guidance.

title: Add intake package scaffolding and exploration seed context helpers
description: Provide an executable intake package generator plus intake and exploration context helpers so project rules and user-provided intake materials can be assembled into a concrete pre-exploration input bundle.
close_reason: Implemented rules bundle, intake package helpers, command wiring, template updates, and verified helper outputs

title: Add executable helper for FlowForge project rules context
description: Add a thin CLI helper that reads the installed project rule bundle in canonical order and emits a deterministic context block, then wire it into the managed script bundle and documentation.

title: Introduce a canonical rule-loading protocol for FlowForge adapters
description: Extract the project rule bundle loading order into a single canonical guide and update skill and command surfaces to reference that guide instead of repeating the same instructions in every wrapper.

title: Load project seed rules into FlowForge skill and commands
description: Ensure the installed project seed rules bundle is explicitly read by the FlowForge skill and command surfaces before workflow actions, so project rules actually participate in exploration, proposal, archive, and status reasoning.

title: Collapse model template parts into a single document
description: Simplify the default model template so projects copy and edit one complete document instead of coordinating a split parts directory. Update the surrounding guidance and remove the default parts-based reading path.

title: Split workflow guidance into core docs and project seed rules
description: Audit existing FlowForge workflow guidance, keep stable platform mechanisms in core, and move project-default working guidance into a seed rules template that is installed into projects.

title: Freeze core conclusions for input package and workflow rules
description: Capture the currently agreed conclusions about input packages, workflow-level rules, and the split between core FlowForge behavior and project-installed seed rules before decomposing existing workflow content.

title: Define Obsidian-compatible frontmatter for FlowForge docs
description: Design and implement per-document frontmatter metadata for FlowForge markdown artifacts, keep body content substantive, and update workflow docs/templates/validation so explorations, proposals, designs, models, findings, and conventions each expose their own minimal attributes while meta.yaml remains proposal-level only.
close_reason: Document frontmatter workflow implemented and smoke-validated

title: Upgrade FlowForge ownership summaries and exploration validation
description: Complete the missing parts of today's FlowForge upgrade: add human-readable ownership summaries to canonical templates, extend generator and validators, add exploration validation, and realign the data-service exploration/proposal docs in saas-service.
